zh.Of, Ashington, , England: 英國英格蘭薩默塞特阿生頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Saint Barbe of Ashington於1460年出生在英國薩默塞特阿生頓時，他的父親John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset31歲，母親Jane Sydenham27歲。他於1489年與Margery Ann Grey在英國薩默塞特爾頓 康特洛結婚。他們至少是13個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1499年在英國薩默塞特享年39歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特阿生頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Saint Barbe of Ashington于1460年出生在英国薩默塞特阿生頓时，他的父亲John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset31岁，母亲Jane Sydenham27岁。他于1489年与Margery Ann Grey在英国薩默塞特爾頓 康特洛结婚。他们至少是13个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1499年在英国薩默塞特享年39岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特阿生頓。

nameMeanings.name: Barbe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Laurent, Andre, Gillis, Jean Pierre, Serge, Sylvie, Thierry, Yves.
French: descriptive nickname for someone with a beard, Old French barbe (from Latin barba).
French (Barbé): adjectival derivative of 1, meaning ‘bearded’.

variants.name: Barb
meanings: German: variant of   Barbe  . This surname is also found in France (mainly Alsace).
Romanian: variant of   Barbu  .

variants.name: Barbier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Emile, Pierre, Alexandre, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Laurette, Manon, Marcel, Marcellin, Michel.
French: occupational name for a barber-surgeon (see Barber ), Old French barbier (from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). Compare Barbe and Barbee .
German: occupational name for a barber, from Middle High German barbierer, or an occupational name for an armorer, from barbier(e) ‘metal visor (to protect the face)’.

variants.name: Barbon
meanings: English: habitational name from Barbon in Westmorland. The placename is probably from Old Norse bjórr ‘beaver’ + brunnr ‘stream’, alternatively from Old English bera ‘bear’ + burna ‘stream’.
English: nickname from Middle English bare bon ‘bare bone’, perhaps denoting a thin or bare-legged man.
French and English (of French Huguenot origin): nickname for a bearded man, from an augmentative of French   Barbe  .

variants.name: Barby
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Merwin, Otto, Wolfgang.  French Celestine, Pierre.
German: habitational name from a place so named in Saxony-Anhalt.
French (mainly Dordogne): derivative of   Barbe   1.

variants.name: Barbin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Angelle, Marcel.
French: diminutive of   Barbe   1.

variants.name: Barbot
meanings: French:
from the Old French female personal name Barbot, a diminutive of Barbe , a short form of Barbara .
nickname for someone who tended to speak indistinctly, from French barboter ‘to babble’, or for someone with a beard, from a diminutive of Old French barbe ‘beard’.

variants.name: Barbay
meanings: French: variant of Barbé (see Barbe ).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Ashington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: (i) locative name from any of various places called Ashington (especially Lincs, Sussex), or Ashingdon (Essex). (ii) possibly a variant of Ashenden in Kent contexts; note Robt. Ashinden, 1730 in IGI (Deal, Kent).

variants.name: IGI
meanings: 1 Scottish: perhaps a variant of Kelvey (a reduced form of McKelvey ), for the two names appear in the same parishes in SW Scotland and IoM; compare Jon Kelvie, 1697 in IGI (Lezayre, IoM); Grisal Kelvie, 1777 in IGI (Greenock, Renfrews). However, the acquisition of additional -n is difficult to explain. It may in fact be a reduced form of McKelvin , for which there is little historical evidence. There is no known connection with the River Kelvin or with the modern settlement of Kelvinside in Glasgow.
2  English: unexplained.

variants.name: Ashenden
meanings: English: locative name from Ashenden in Tenterden (Kent), which is recorded as Esserinden' in 1278.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: name bestowed in honour of the medieval poet Dante Alighieri ( 1265–1321 ), also used in the English-speaking world. As a medieval given name Dante was a contracted form of Durante ‘steadfast, enduring’. In an English context it was famously borne by the pre-Raphaelite poet and painter Dante Gabriel Rossetti ( 1828–1882 ), the son of an exiled Italian scholar and patriot.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Saint Barbe of Ashington was born in 1460, in Ashington, Somerset, England, his father, John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset, was 31 and his mother, Jane Sydenham, was 27. He married Margery Ann Grey in 1489, in Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 13 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1499, in Somerset, England, at the age of 39, and was buried in Ashington, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Saint Barbe of Ashington est né en 1460, à Ashington, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Jane Sydenham, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Margery Ann Grey en 1489, à Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 13 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1499, à Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterré à Ashington, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Saint Barbe of Ashington 1460 in Ashington, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset 31 und seine Mutter Jane Sydenham 27. Er hat 1489 in Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England Margery Ann Grey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 13 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1499 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Somerset, England und wurde in Ashington, Somerset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Saint Barbe of Ashington è nato il nel 1460 nel Ashington, Somerset, England, il suo padre, John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Jane Sydenham, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Margery Ann Grey nel 1489, nel Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 13 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1499, nel Somerset, England, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolto a Ashington, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Saint Barbe of Ashingtonが1460年にイギリスソメルセトアシュイントンで生まれた時，父John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorsetは31歳，母Jane Sydenhamは27歳であった。1489年にイギリスソメルセトチルトン カンテロでMargery Ann Greyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも13人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1499年にイギリスソメルセトで39歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトアシュイントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Saint Barbe of Ashington(이)가 1460년 영국 서머세트 애신그턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Sydenham(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1489년 영국 서머세트 치턴 캉로에서 Margery Ann Grey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1499년 영국 서머세트에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 애신그턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Saint Barbe of Ashington nasceu em 1460, em Ashington, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Jane Sydenham, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery Ann Grey em 1489, em Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 13 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1499, em Somerset, Inglaterra, com 39 anos, e foi sepultado em Ashington, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Saint Barbe of Ashington родился в 1460 г. в местности ашингтон, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Jane Sydenham, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery Ann Grey в 1489 г. в местности чилтон кантелоу, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 13 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1499 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 39 лет и был похоронен в местности ашингтон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Saint Barbe of Ashington nació en 1460, en Ashington, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, John Saint Barbe Sheriff of Somerset and Dorset, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Jane Sydenham, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Margery Ann Grey en 1489, en Chilton Cantelo, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 13 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1499, en Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultado en Ashington, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
